Look, quality questions. Quality questions control this cycle. This is fact-finding, qualifying. This is a mistake that so many people making cold calls, they get in their name, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, and they don't know anything when the call is over. If you never ask a question, you won't know what the problem is, and then you can't make the big claims, set the big hooks, shine, you know, use the lures that they're going to respond to. You've got to know what their problem is. So look, once you have somebody on the phone and they haven't hung up on you yet, you've made your greeting, it's time to immediately jump into qualifying to see if they even have a problem that you can fix. Fact-finding will uncover what I call the BBM or Dominant Buying Motive. The reason why someone would buy something, okay? This jacket. I didn't buy this jacket because I needed a jacket.
